description: Complete vanilla JS calculator with session history
intent: Build a complete calculator as a single-page web app: HTML layout, CSS styling, JS arithmetic engine with operator precedence, button-driven UI, and a session history panel. Each teammate builds their own version independently on their own branch. Best overall implementation wins.
notes: Each person builds in trial-calculator/ on their own branch (feat/task-01-a, feat/task-01-b, feat/task-01-c). Target files: index.html, css/styles.css, js/calculator.js, js/ui.js, js/history.js
acceptance_checks: Handles edge cases: -5+3=-2 (unary minus), 10/3 returns decimal, 1/0 doesn't crash
Calculator is centered and looks intentional on a typical laptop screen (>=1280px wide)
Buttons have visible hover and active states
Display has clear visual separation from the button grid
Clicking buttons updates the display; pressing = evaluates and shows the result
A Clear button resets the display
Each evaluation appends to a visible history panel (expression + result, in-memory only)
Invalid expressions (e.g. 2++3) show an error without crashing the page
Opens in a browser by double-clicking index.html, no local server needed (no ES modules, no fetch calls)

submissions.author: person_c
submissions.notes: Vanilla JS calculator with parser-based evaluation, session history, and polished local-file UI.

comparison_notes: Amal's calculator selected as the winner during dress rehearsal review.
